The address doesn't adapt to regional address standards. While in Belgium and the Netherlands, the building number follows the street, in North America, the custom is that the number precedes the street in the first line of the address. This might cause some small confusion and would help if remedied.

I do not know if it’s country specific thing but in Bulgaria when you send letters, the sender names and addresses are in top left corner and the recipient is in the bottom right, stamp is on top right of the envelope. Nevertheless, maybe not necessarily needed but it’s adding personal touch to it. One more suggestion, you can experiment with handwriting fonts customisations, will be not the same as the personal handwriting but still is getting close to the overall idea of the letters :) Map page is a bit hard to navigate (personally) and to check which countries are filled.
